page: Amazon Web Services z1d.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring high-frequency compute resources and local NVMe storage. Powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers 8 vCPUs across 4 physical Intel Xeon 8151 cores with hyper-threading enabled. The dedicated CPU allocation operates at a high base clock speed of 4.0 GHz, supported by 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core.

The instance includes 300 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, providing low-latency local disk access. Network performance is characterized by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512F, AES cryptography, and BMI2, which assist in accelerating specialized mathematical and cryptographic operations.

Benchmark results position the z1d.2xlarge in the average tier across multiple metrics. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1320.0 and a multi-core score of 5016.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 9862.94. Memory bandwidth tests show 487.39 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 52.49 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Database operations perform steadily, with Redis SET operations reaching 8.47 million operations per second. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-parameter LLM inference show weaker relative performance. This profile makes the instance highly suitable for relational databases, high-frequency electronic design automation, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads requiring high single-core performance.
description: Amazon Web Services z1d.2xlarge is a memory-optimized, high-frequency instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 8 vCPUs from a dedicated 4.0 GHz Intel Xeon 8151 processor, paired with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 300 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it supports AVX-512F and AES instructions. Benchmark data indicates solid performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1320.0, a Passmark CPU score of 9862.94, and Redis SET operations reaching 8.47 million operations per second. While multi-threaded decompression and larger LLM inference tasks show lower relative efficiency, its high clock speed and 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio make it highly effective for relational databases, electronic design automation, and memory-bound workloads requiring strong single-thread execution.
og_description: Amazon Web Services z1d.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs at 4.0 GHz, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and 300 GB local NVMe SSD storage, optimized for database and high-frequency workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services z1d.2xlarge features 8 vCPUs at 4.0 GHz, 64 GB RAM, and 300 GB local NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-optimized compute platform featuring dedicated high-clock processors and integrated local NVMe SSD storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8151 processor running at a high 4.0 GHz clock speed.
64.0 GB of DDR4 RAM providing a high 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio.
300 GB of low-latency local NVMe SSD instance storage.
Delivers 8.47 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks.
Best suited for relational databases, EDA, and high-frequency single-threaded applications.
